content: Hey Saturday, quick prep for our Solstice work session tomorrow. Can you help me outline what we need to cover?

Absolutely. I’d structure it around the four core modules: PTO accrual rules, biannual performance reviews with 360 feedback, org chart visualization, and Slack team announcements. Since Nadia Kowalski is PM, we should also add a short decision log section for her open questions.
Yep, makes sense. For PTO, I want to confirm the policy details are reflected correctly in Solstice before we demo.

Got it—Solstice should show accrual at 1.5 days per month and cap carryover at 15 days into the next year. We should test at least two scenarios: a new hire mid-year and an employee with excess unused balance at year-end. I can draft those test cases for the session notes.
Perfect. Also, I keep getting questions about review cadence and who can submit feedback.

We can clarify that reviews run biannually, and each cycle includes 360 feedback from peers plus manager input. In the session, let’s confirm role-based permissions so employees, managers, and HRBPs see the right forms. I’d also verify cycle dates are visible in the employee dashboard.
Nice. Last thing: Nadia wants org chart + Slack integration covered in 15 min. Can we keep that tight?

Yes—5 minutes for org chart visualization (reporting lines, department filters, vacancy visibility) and 10 minutes for Slack announcements setup and sample posts. We can demo a team announcement pushed to #people-ops-updates and confirm delivery logs in Solstice. I’ll keep the talking points concise so Nadia can drive decisions quickly.
